title: Copper Founder: Building a Billion Dollar Company & Fixing Crypto Custody
summary: Copper founder Dmitry Tokarev joins Empire live from DAS to reflect on building a $1.25B crypto custody company, the brutal lessons of hypergrowth (36 to 236 employees in a year), and why he stepped down in late 2024 to build his next company Bronze with a lean team under 40 people. He argues the next wave of crypto adoption won't be saved by TradFi institutions — it'll be built by the same crypto-native players who got here, now targeting tokenized real-world assets.
keyTakeaways: Hire slow: throwing headcount at problems is a trap — rapid hiring (nearly 1 person/day at Copper's peak) destroys culture and creates colliding subcultures that become unmanageable

Know your superpower: Tokarev identifies as a builder/product person and learned too late to stop attempting things he wasn't good at — founders should double down on strengths and hire deliberately for gaps
AI is changing hiring calculus now: Bronze stopped hiring after adopting Claude Code ~6 weeks ago, staying under 40 people — a real-time example of AI compressing team size
Zero-rate era distorted the industry: cheap capital forced custody players into marketing arms races (Copper vs. Fireblocks, Anchorage, BitGo) that had nothing to do with building a better product

FTX collapse was partly a scaling failure: Tokarev's firsthand account suggests SBF lost operational control with 130+ direct reports before the fraud became irreversible — scale without structure is existential risk
